reasoning: The question asks whether at least one European country or the EU will formally announce a commitment to provide Ukraine with HIMARS-compatible rocket artillery systems or functional substitutes by 31 December 2025. Substitutes explicitly listed in the question include the SAMP/T. Web searches reveal that several such declarations have allegedly already occurred or are deeply in motion. Specifically, multiple sources published in mid-May 2025 (e.g., Militarnyi on May 19, 2025; UNITED24 Media on May 20, 2025) confirm that Italy is sending a *new* (third) SAMP/T air defense system to Ukraine, expected to arrive in fall 2025, along with 400 M113 vehicles. Though SAMP/T is largely an air defense system (which usually differs from HIMARS), the prompt explicitly lists SAMP/T as a valid substitute system ("Substitute systems (e.g., South Korea’s K239 Chunmoo, PULS, or SAMP/T)"), which means this Italian pledge for SAMP/T definitively satisfies the resolution criteria. Aside from SAMP/T, the precedent of European nations heavily supplying M270s and even purchasing HIMARS directly from US stocks (Germany in May 2024) indicates a very high base rate for European commitments of this nature. Furthermore, European nations are actively exploring local equivalents, such as a Franco-German pivot to Chunmoo or GMARS. Combining the extreme likelihood of Europe keeping Ukraine supplied with these pivotal long-range systems, and the direct evidence that Italy committed in May 2025 to sending another SAMP/T system (a system explicitly designated as a "substitute" in the question's criteria), the probability of resolving Yes is extremely high. Given minor uncertainty about whether the Italian SAMP/T pledge qualifies perfectly (e.g., determining if it was a formal enough announcement or if the question's strange inclusion of SAMP/T will be re-judged due to the "comparable to U.S.-made HIMARS" definition clause that conflicts with SAMP/T being air defense), I will limit my forecast to 89%.
